content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule structure. Looking at atom N:2 (nitrogen with index 2), I can see its covalent connections:

1. C:1 (carbon atom with index 1) - connected by a blue bond
2. N:3 (nitrogen atom with index 3) - connected by a blue bond  
3. H:19 (hydrogen atom with index 19) - connected by a blue bond

The image clearly shows these three atoms directly bonded to N:2. There are no other atoms directly connected to N:2.

Regarding the geomconf_index, based on the file naming convention in the working directory, I can see a file named "geomconf_186.xyz", which suggests the geomconf_index is 186.

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 2 (N) are:
- Atom index 1 (C)
- Atom index 3 (N)
- Atom index 19 (H)

And the geomconf_index is 186.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 2, whose element should be N. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
